The Humboldt County Commission on Human Rights is hereby established.

228-2. 
AIJl'HORITY FOR ESTABLISHMENT. 
The Commission is established pursuant to Title 5, Division 1, Part 
1, Chapter 1, Article 10 of the Government Code (§t 50260-50265) and Sec-

(Ord. 1023 § 2, 4/22/75) 
228-3. 
PURPOSE OF CXAYf"ITSSICN. 
The Commission is established to aid in the eradication of dis-
crimination in Humboldt County, with particular reference to housing, 
employment, education and public accommodation. 
The Board of Supervisors 
recognizes that such discrimination is harmful to those discriminated 
against and is ultimately harmful to the County, as a whole, due to the 
lack of utilization of the human potential of those subject to such dis-
crimination, and further due to depreciated property values and the 
increased cost of governmental services such as law enforcement and 
welfare. 
Committees may be established by the Comnlission. 
(Ord. 1023 
§ 5, 4/22/75)

The Commission shall consist of fifteen (15) members who shall be 
appointed by the Board of Supervisors. 
The Supervisors of each district 
shall appoint two (2) members, and the remaining five (5) members shall 
be appointed at large. 
The two (2) members appointed by the individual 
Supervisors of Districts 1, 2 and 3 shall serve a term which shall become 
effective on the date the member is appointed and shall expire on June JO, 
1977.

MEETINGS. 
TheCow~ission shall hold regular monthly meetings at such ti~e and 
place as shall be agreed upon by the Commission and such other special 
meetings as shall be required from time to time. 
Special meetings shall 
be .::a11ed by the Chairman of the Cow~ission or, in his absence from the

Special meetings shall be effective for the

notice shall contain the subject of the special meeting. 
No action taken 
at either regular or special meetings ~ha11 be yalid unless approved by a 
majority of the members of the Commission present and voting, and unless 
a quorum is present. 
A quorum shall consist of five (5) members of the 
Commission. 
(Ord. 1023 § 8, 4/22/75) 
In the event that any member of ·the Commission shall absent himself 
from three (3) consecutive regular meetings of the Commission without 
showing good cause therefor to the Chairman, at the fourth such consecu-
tive regular meeting from which he is absent his place shall automatically 
become vacant. 
Such vacancy shall be reported to the Board of Super-
visors, and the Board of Supervisors shall appoint another member for 
the unexpired term of the member whose absence gave rise to the vacancy. 
(Ord. 1023 § 9, 4/22/75)

sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act therefore does not prohibit one from holding public office merely because one has financial interests. It merely requires that once one assumes public office, one must refrain from participating in decisions which would affect one's private interests. Also, note that the Political Reform Act does not create an incompatibility of offices based on receipt of income or employment from any agency.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a public official position held by someone with certain financial interests create a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act of 1974?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit holding public office based on financial interests, but requires recusal from decisions affecting those interests; no incompatibility of offices is created based on income or employment from agencies.

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act therefore does not prohibit one from holding public office merely because one has financial interests. It merely requires that once one assumes public office, one must refrain from participating in decisions which would affect one's private interests. Also, note that the Political Reform Act does not create an incompatibility of offices based on receipt of income or employment from any agency.
